steps.title: Find the slope of line k from two points on the graph
steps.content:
From the graph, line $k$ passes through $(0, 2)$ and $(4, -3)$.
Using the slope formula:
$$m_k = \frac{-3 - 2}{4 - 0} = \frac{-5}{4}$$

steps.title: Find the slope of line j using perpendicularity
steps.content:
When two lines are perpendicular, the product of their slopes equals $-1$:
$$m_k \cdot m_j = -1$$
$$-\frac{5}{4} \cdot m_j = -1$$
$$m_j = \frac{4}{5}$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept using point-slope form
steps.content:
Line $j$ has slope $\frac{4}{5}$ and passes through $(19, 23)$:
$$y - 23 = \frac{4}{5}(x - 19)$$
Set $x = 0$ to find the $y$-intercept:
$$y - 23 = \frac{4}{5}(0 - 19) = -\frac{76}{5}$$
$$y = 23 - \frac{76}{5} = \frac{115}{5} - \frac{76}{5} = \frac{39}{5}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
